tldr: OpenIAP is a cross-platform IAP specification, not a runtime; Balancy is a no-code LiveOps and monetization platform. The migration is really about adding an economy, offers, and A/B testing layer on top of whichever OpenIAP-compliant library you use.
why_switch: Manage virtual economy in a CMS
Run A/B tests on pricing and bundles
Let designers ship without code deploys
export_method: OpenIAP is a spec; there is no service-side state to export. Inventory the implementing library you use (e.g. react-native-iap, flutter_inapp_purchase, or another OpenIAP-compliant SDK) and document where in your codebase it is called. List product IDs from App Store Connect and Google Play Console — these stay vendor-neutral. If your app already has a virtual economy modeled in your own code or backend, export its schema (items, currencies, bundles) since this is what will move into Balancy. Capture any custom A/B test logic for bundles or pricing so you can recreate experiments in Balancy. Document analytics events fired around purchases so the funnel survives the migration.
import_method: Create a Balancy project and define your economy in the CMS: items, currencies, bundles, and store pages mapped to existing store SKUs. Keep your OpenIAP-compliant library as the IAP transaction layer — Balancy is not a replacement for the actual purchase API. Install Balancy's SDK for your platform (or use REST if a first-class SDK is unavailable) and load catalog data from Balancy at runtime. Wire purchase success callbacks from your IAP library into Balancy event tracking. Replace hardcoded catalog screens with Balancy-driven content. Keep server-side receipt validation intact. Roll out behind a feature flag and phase the change.

concept_mapping.target: Balancy item linked to store SKU

effort: 2-5 weeks depending on catalog size and economy depth
gotchas: OpenIAP is a spec, not a transaction layer — you still need the library that implements it.
Balancy does not replace receipt validation; keep server-side checks intact.
Verify Balancy SDK availability for your stack (RN, Flutter, native, Unity).
Subscription lifecycle still flows through stores and your IAP library.
Concept mismatch: OpenIAP is transactional; Balancy adds economy and LiveOps on top.
Existing analytics dashboards must be remapped to Balancy's event schema.

email.subject: Adding Balancy on top of our OpenIAP setup — 14-day plan
email.body:
Hi team,

We plan to introduce Balancy alongside our OpenIAP-aligned IAP integration over the next 14 days. The IAP library stays as the transaction layer; Balancy will own the catalog, offers, and A/B testing.

Proposed timeline:
Days 1-2: Inventory current IAP usage — product IDs, purchase flow, server validation, analytics, any custom catalog logic.
Days 3-5: Model the economy in Balancy CMS: items, currencies, bundles, store pages mapped to store SKUs.
Days 6-9: Integrate the Balancy SDK (or REST) and replace hardcoded catalog screens with Balancy-driven content.
Days 10-12: Wire purchase callbacks into Balancy events; run an A/B test on a small bundle to validate.
Days 13-14: QA on all platforms, regression-test receipt validation, ship behind a feature flag.

Receipt validation stays on our backend. Please flag any custom economy or bundle logic that needs CMS-ification.

Thanks,
Your customer
